verses.text: Wisdom cries aloud at the gates and the crossroads, calling to all men, even the simple and foolish, to gain understanding. She offers herself freely and publicly. God's wisdom withholds itself from none who will listen.

Wisdom cries aloud at the gates and the crossroads, calling to all men, even the simple and foolish, to gain understanding. She offers herself freely and publicly. God's wisdom withholds itself from none who will listen.

She speaks 'excellent things,' all in righteousness, with nothing froward or perverse, plain to him who understands. Her words are true and upright. The teaching of true wisdom is honest and clear, never crooked.

She speaks 'excellent things,' all in righteousness, with nothing froward or perverse, plain to him who understands. Her words are true and upright. The teaching of true wisdom is honest and clear, never crooked.

She speaks 'excellent things,' all in righteousness, with nothing froward or perverse, plain to him who understands. Her words are true and upright. The teaching of true wisdom is honest and clear, never crooked.

'Receive my instruction, and not silver... for wisdom is better than rubies,' and nothing desired compares with her. Wisdom outvalues all wealth. The treasure she gives is worth more than anything the world prizes.

'Receive my instruction, and not silver... for wisdom is better than rubies,' and nothing desired compares with her. Wisdom outvalues all wealth. The treasure she gives is worth more than anything the world prizes.

Wisdom dwells with prudence, hates pride and evil, and gives counsel and strength, so that 'by me kings reign' and rulers decree justice. She governs good government. All right authority and sound counsel flow from the wisdom of God.

'I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me,' and she leads them to durable riches and righteousness. Wisdom rewards her seekers. Those who pursue her early inherit a wealth that lasts.

'The LORD possessed me in the beginning of his way,' Wisdom says, before the earth, depths, hills, or fields were made. She is from everlasting. This personified Wisdom points to the eternal Son, with God before creation.

'The LORD possessed me in the beginning of his way,' Wisdom says, before the earth, depths, hills, or fields were made. She is from everlasting. This personified Wisdom points to the eternal Son, with God before creation.

When God prepared the heavens, set the sea's bound, and laid earth's foundations, Wisdom was there 'as one brought up with him,' His daily delight, rejoicing before Him. She shared in creation's joy. Christ, the Wisdom of God, was the Father's eternal delight.

'Now therefore hearken unto me,' Wisdom pleads; blessed are they who watch daily at her gates, for 'whoso findeth me findeth life,' but 'he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul.' To find wisdom is to find life. To reject her is self-destruction.
